title: Chapter 2
text: To the Mother of Us All To my mother And to my children I owe many thanks to many people for their help in making this book a reality, after so long. In particular, I would like to thank Michall Jeffers and John Warner, for bringing Hamlet’s Mill to my attention; Giorgio de Santillana and Hertha von Dechend, authors of Hamlet’s Mill; Barbara Luedtke; Jim Frenkel; Vernor Vinge; Brian Thomsen; the Clarion West class of ‘88; Deborah Kahn Cunningham; Lolly Boyer; Steve and Julia Sabbagh; Merrilee Heifetz; and Richard Plantagenet, King of England, who may be the most misunderstood man in history. AUTHOR’S NOTE The following names of characters and places are pronounced as shown: Ananke (Uh-NONkee) Arienrhod (AIRY-en-rode) Danaquil Lu (DAN-uh-keel LOO) Gundhalinu (Gun-dahLEEnoo) Jerusha PalaThion (Jer-OO-shuh PAL-uhTHY-un) Kedalion Niburu (Keh-DAY-lee-un Nih-BUR-oo) Kharemough (KAREuhmoff) Kharemoughi (KAREuhMAWG-ee) Kullervo (KulLAIRvoh) Miroe Ngenet (MIR-row EngEN-it) Mundilfoere (MUNdil-fair) Sandhi (SAHNdee) Tiamat (TEE-uhmaht) Tuo Ne’el (TOO-oh NEEL) Vhanu (VAHnoo)
